notes: kiro-cli synchronous no-LLM slash command (/help, /session-id). kiro's Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir:.kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}: the daemon tails the <uuid>.jsonl. The parser emits state only for Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear; a synchronous slash appends NOTHING to the transcript (the live smoke .jsonl has zero slash literals — .build/refresh/kiro-cli-smoke/), so the tailer fires no event and the session stays ready. A warm-up 'warm' turn drives ready->working->ready to materialize the <uuid>.jsonl so resolve_transcript can bind. After warm_turn_end returns to ready, the slash_window phase pins the session ready with duration_at_least_ms:4000 and invariants forbidding state_transition to working/waiting — those are what would fire if /help or /session-id (incorrectly) drove a model call. The trailing 'alive' turn drives a SECOND ready->working->ready cycle proving the session is still alive (the scenario's liveness arc). RECORDABLE via the TUI-only driver: drive-kiro-cli-interactive.sh elicits send/slash/wait_turn/sleep (DRIVE_ELICITS) and delivers the slash straight to the live kiro-cli TUI via tmux send-keys (NOT headless --no-interactive, which writes no session file). No wait_turn follows a slash (a no-LLM slash writes no text-only AssistantMessage, so turn_count never increments). The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (those live only in the <uuid>.json sidecar the v1 parser does not read), so observations assert model:unknown, cost_nonzero:false, tokens_nonzero:false.

text: kiro-cli session (the minted <uuid>) appears in ready within 2s of launch
Warm-up 'warm' prompt drives ready -> working (real LLM turn; materializes the <uuid>.jsonl so subsequent steps can resolve_transcript)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready (same <uuid> session id)

Spec: state stays ready throughout the no-LLM slash commands /help and /session-id — neither appends a line to the .jsonl, so the tailer fires no event and no working/waiting transition is recorded for the slash command itself
Trailing 'alive' prompt drives ready -> working a SECOND time on the same <uuid> session — proves the session is still alive after the slash window (scenario liveness arc)
Trailing turn completes back to ready — final state ready (state topology ready->working->ready)
